question_text: A planet has a gravitational field strength of 3.7 N/kg. An astronaut has a mass of 80 kg. Calculate the weight of the astronaut on this planet.
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula W = m × g, where m is mass in kg and g is gravitational field strength in N/kg. So 80 kg × 3.7 N/kg = 296 N. This is the gravitational force pulling the astronaut downwards on that planet.
